Siyanda Resources · Csi Monthly Report 2025
Community Social Investment
Initiatives across three core pillars delivering sustained community assistance across KwaZulu-Natal.
Nutrition Centres
Soup kitchens providing essential sustenance to families lacking regular meal access across Jozini, KZN.
Basic Education Support
School facilities, uniforms, stationery and tertiary bursaries — 417 students funded since 2011.
Skills Development
Robotics, AI and vocational programmes enabling community members to find employment and contribute positively.
14Active distribution centres
340Families fed monthly
58Volunteers
Nutrition Centre Donations — Jozini, KZN
Feeding Families Across Three Centres

SR has donated to several nutrition centres over the past three years — covering groceries, hygiene essentials and fresh bread delivered twice weekly to families in need.

"No man can be wise on an empty stomach."
— George Eliot

Education &Amp; Infrastructure Highlights
School Ablution Refurbishment
A R40.2M sanitation initiative across schools in Jouberton, Klerksdorp and KZN. 26 community volunteers maintain facilities daily. Phase 1 completed 31 May 2023.

Section 02 & 03 · Siyanda Bakgatla Platinum Mine — Slp2 Close-Out &Amp; Slp3 Implementation
02
Sbpm · Slp2 Close-Out Report 2024
Led Progress — 2016–2020 Social Labour Plan
Major capital investments in school infrastructure, libraries and healthcare across the Bojanala Platinum District.
Northam Library
Fully refurbished and re-equipped — new bookshelves, study desks and a conference room serving the Northam community as a learning and civic hub.
Tshomankana Secondary School
Targeted infrastructure improvements to create safer, more functional spaces for teaching and learning — keys handed over to school leadership.

03
Sbpm · Slp3 Implementation Report 2025
Led Budget Vs Actual — 2021–2025
Targeted investment in community infrastructure across Northam, Disake and Mononono. Total spend: R19.9M.
Ambulance Donation — Limpopo Dept Of Health
A fully equipped Limpopo EMS ambulance, proudly sponsored by SBPM — ensuring faster emergency response in under-resourced areas. Cost: R881 394.
Mononono Clinic Upgrade
New roof, full render, improved waiting areas and ablutions. Cost: R4 776 880. Original scope 100% — punch list 98% complete.

Section 04 · Siyanda Chrome Investment — Sci Community Report 2024–2026
04
Siyanda Chrome Investment · Community Report
Building Community Through Shared Moments
Social engagement, healthcare, education and infrastructure investment across Limpopo & North West communities surrounding SCI operations.
Sports & Community — 27 Feb 2024
Siyanda Wildebeest Rugby — Ampie Le Roux Cup

Sponsoring local sport strengthens community ties and supports local talent. SCI sponsored new rugby gear (R14 261) for the inaugural Ampie le Roux Cup — an event that has since become a community fixture.

Closing Statement
Across each of these projects, a common thread emerges — a deep commitment to restoring dignity, expanding opportunity, and strengthening the fabric of community life. From classrooms and clinics to libraries and sanitation facilities, these investments touch everyday experiences, shaping how learners learn, how patients are cared for, and how communities access essential services.
